# The data

**All of it is synthetic.** Every transcript in these files was generated by
`make_data.py` from sentence templates. No person said any of these words. There is no
IRB protocol, no consent form, and no PHI here — which is exactly why we can spend three
hours pasting it into chatbots.

It is designed to *behave* like a real picture-description dataset: severity degrades
lexical content, the group difference is real but not absurd, and there is one measure
(`type_token_ratio`) that looks useful and mostly isn't. That last one is on purpose.

## transcripts.csv — 60 rows, one per participant

| Column | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `participant_id` | chr | `C###` control, `A###` aphasia |
| `group` | chr | `control` or `aphasia` |
| `age` | int | years |
| `sex` | chr | `F` / `M` |
| `education_years` | int | years of formal education |
| `months_post_onset` | dbl | months since stroke; `NA` for controls |
| `wab_aq` | dbl | Aphasia Quotient, 0–100. Higher = less impaired. Controls sit at ~99 (ceiling) |
| `transcript` | chr | their spoken description of the picture |

## transcripts_long.csv — 60 rows, 30 participants × 2 visits

The aphasia group only, measured `acute` (~1 month post-onset) and `chronic`
(~12 months). Same columns plus `timepoint`. **These are repeated measures** — 60 rows,
30 people. Any model that ignores that is wrong.

## features.csv — 60 rows, the answers to Part 1

The NLP measures pre-computed, so the R half works even if you never finished the Python
half. Columns: `n_words`, `n_unique_words`, `type_token_ratio`, `mean_word_length`,
`content_word_ratio`, `filler_rate`.

## What's actually in the signal

Within the aphasia group, correlations with WAB-AQ:

| Measure | r |
|---|---|
| `filler_rate` | **−0.83** |
| `n_words` | +0.60 |
| `content_word_ratio` | +0.51 |
| `mean_word_length` | +0.46 |
| `type_token_ratio` | +0.17 ← the trap |

Type-token ratio falls as transcripts get longer, and impaired speakers produce shorter
transcripts. So TTR is fighting itself. It is a real, well-documented confound, and no AI
assistant will mention it unless you ask.
